reviews.review_description:
There where 5 problems that made the experience less then ideal. I know it's probably astronomicaly hard to make a VR game, so no judgment from me, and no offense meant. But there where t issues large enough to make me not want to play again. 

1. Rockets. Every time I died it was from a rocket. They go through walls, spawn out of bounds, and keep you from being able to traverse the environment. Unless I'm seriously missing something, which is possible, you basically just have to hide behind a wall and hope splash damage doesn't get you through walls. Maybe they spawned out of bounds every time and that caused issues, but it seems like shooting them didn't do anything and there was no way to defeat them. 

2. Enemy movement. Enemys move quickly and tend to RUSH you HARD. It makes every battle a face-to-face fight, and you can just hide behind a door with a knife, and that seems to be the most effective way to play the game. On easy mode, if I left the safety of the bottle neck in my bathroom, I had guys face to face with me immediately. This leads me to my next problem 

3. Collision. Because of gun collision and hit boxes, when enemies where within arms reach, I would raise my handgun to defend myself, and it wouldn't be there. It would snag on the enemy in front of me and stay pinned pointed downwards. I spent most of the game body-blocking and holding my gun up and back and up, shooting down past my ear to hit anything. Collision may be realistic, but for a vr experience like this one, it causes a lot of dissonance with cqb. Which, again, because of the speed of the enemies, this game is all about. 

4. Enemy size. Enemies need scaled down by about 30%. They may be close to realistically sized, but in a vr environment they take up entire doorways and because of the rushing, they take up your entire field of view. It's like shooting at a barn door. I'm pretty sure I hit every shot I fired.

5. Locked content. I got this game specifically to play around with mixed reality. Locking sandbox mode behind level completion seems antithetical to the medium of 'mixed reality, home invasion play space. A large reason I paid for and downloaded this game was to show my friends how cool mixed reality was, and I was hoping to have much more control over the experience. I'm not slogging through level after level, just to be able to hopefully be able to place spawners and disable rockets. I say hopefully, because that's what I assume is inn the sandbox mode. I don't know because there are no details about what it actually is. I'm just assuming and possibly making an as out of u and me.

The new Continuous Mesh Scanning is really great — noticeably faster and, to me, a real step toward the future of gaming. It’s not perfect yet, but you can clearly see the direction this technology is heading. I don’t know any other game that implements this so well. Really great game, and thanks to the developer for always integrating the latest techniques.

Also: we should absolutely support developers like this, because they make cutting-edge tech accessible and usable for everyone.

P.S.: For future continous mesh updates, multiple floors, uneven terrain, and saving/caching the mesh would be awesome.

reviews.review_title: Good but not what you might hope for
reviews.review_description: You can use this as a cool home invasion fantasy simulator. But its not really useable beyond that as the only thing the bots know how to do is run straight toward you. Its not really fun per say but it did help me get more comfortable with shooting people in my home.
I love the game, it can definitely use some work in my opinion but I love the game so far.

Rockets need to be fixed

Game Mechanics are great and physics but please fix the rockets they go through the walls. 

Please add weapon skins
I like the skins but can add like multiple and please 🙏 add an option for realistic weapons skins like the ones from today.

Make it so the bots don’t spawn on top of the table please.

Overall the game is good and very fun but I would love for there to be an option for the realistic weapons skin please like from this date.

reviews.review_title: Great game and even better dev team
reviews.review_description: It’s hard but a lot of fun, and the devs are very helpful!
I love this game so much

reviews.review_title: I am sorry for the negative review
reviews.review_description: I will try the game with new updates my friend soon and I hope the game be better and I apologize for the negative criticism, the game has good specifications and I believe any problems will be fixed if only you could add real weapons in today's appearance in addition to the futuristic weapons

I'm gonna start by saying this game is great. It's very innovative and from what I heard, there's only one guy working on it, so...respect to you! As a fellow programmer, I can only be impressed.

However, I have two main complaints about this game:

1) The ENEMY SPAWN MECHANICS: I can understand that since some are gonna have a very small playing area, sometimes, you have no choice but to have enemies spawning in the player's line of sight, HOWEVER, it is something you should ABSOLUTELY AVOID IF YOU CAN HELP IT. I have configured a playing area with 3 large rooms and a bathroom for good measure, yet enemies still pop right into my face. The game would benefit greatly if you'd implement some sort of exclusion zone where enemies cannot spawn around the player. And make it so enemies CANNOT spawn in the player's line of sight IF THERE IS ENOUGH SPACE IN OTHER ROOMS where enemies can safely spawn without breaking immersion.

2) The DIFFICULTY: This game is WAYYYY too hard. Would it be too much to ask to be able to survive for JUST ONE MINUTE in survival ON EASY MODE or ON THE FIRST MISSION without getting obliterated by rocket-wielding drones or turrets that kill you in one hit? These kinds of enemies should NOT EVEN EXIST on such a low difficulty setting. You should reserve them for the harder settings. Seriously, in the first story mission, I am tasked with surviving TWO MINUTES. Should be easy right? Well no. These two minutes FEEL LIKE AN ETERNITY as I desperately try to avoid getting killed by the dozens of soldiers that attack me or those awful turrets. And the only weapons I have to help deal with that are a knife and a pistol...that's PLAIN UNFAIR!!!

Seriously, the difficulty derails the entire experience. If that's what the first mission is, I'd hate to see what the later ones are like...

My solution is this:

1) Reduce the spawn rate of enemies in the first levels a little.

2) Remove anything that shoots rockets from the first few missions and from the easy and normal modes of survival. Places like these are not meant for things that can easily kill you in one hit. Especially if the rockets sometimes glitch through a wall and kill you when they shouldn't have...😕

Now, you might think the solution might be to improve collision detection on rockets, and while it may be true, a much better solution would be: Fewer enemy rockets.


3) You could add mechanics that sometimes make chests appear throughout the play area. These chests could contain various powerups. Some of them could be first-aid kits that heal you, cybernetic body armor that could make you more resistant to enemies who have powerful attacks...like...rockets, for example 😅, or anything else you can think of.


If you implement these 3 points (or at least the first 2), I'm changing my review to 5 star 😁

developer_response.body: Thank you for the detailed review, I will address these items in my update log for the next updates.  For mission mode I have a option to have no reload which will make the missions much easier, but the other game modes do not currently have this option.  I like the idea of powerups for health.  I can address the difficultly through adding no reload and an option for removal of turrets for all game modes.  Currently the turrets cannot be destroyed, but I can make them destructible next update.

reviews.review_description:
The newest update with the multi room works really well, and makes this game much much better. The game overall just seamlessly works and is highly enjoyable. I’ve spent many hours on it already and recommend it completely! 

Theres some tiny polishing that can be done but the developer communicates and is seemingly working on it often to keep the updates coming! 

Some things I’d personally love to see. Better skins for the some of the enemies. Being able to put weapons “back” if you accidentally pick up the wrong one. Some of the guns scaling is too large, I know it’s probably realistic in size but most homes can’t accommodate a mini gun turning around lol. But most of all I really want it co-op and 1v1 capable!!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! I really appreciate it!

reviews.review_description: I really enjoy playing mixed reality games. I just got it today, and it's already reached my top 5 it's super fun to play even by yourself. There are fun weapons to choose from and an overall good time, in my opinion. 10/10, and I recommend it. Thank you.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review!

reviews.review_description: Takes a bit of practice but I like the game either way( ;

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review!

reviews.review_description:
Good and fun FPS for Mixed Reality. Optimized for Q3.

Recommendations! Buy it now!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review!

reviews.review_description: I just got this game on my quest 3. I'm amazed at everything this game has to offer in the comfort of my own home! Though, it could still use more content which is why I give it a 4 for now. We need attachments we can take on and off on the fly and we need more variety of enemies. Something similar to COD MW3 coop/wave based survival mode where every few levels we get new enemies and a boss or two for every 10 waves. Aside from my suggestion this game is really fun. Hoping to see it grow more than a quick MR shooter 🙏

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review!  I look forward to adding more content. Those sound like excellent Ideas!  I hope I can find developers that can help me with with all the updates so I can release them quicker to the players.

reviews.review_description: played in my house and i turn the corner and i get blasted by a enemy

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review!

reviews.review_description: The maximum expression of mixed reality. Different game modes and constant improvements make it mandatory for those who like this genre of games. highly recommend this game!

Great game! You look like a crazy person to anyone around you, but I don't care! 

Easy setup but 
1. Would like the enemies to react and cover eventually

2. Automatic grip on the weapons. Sometimes I let go the grip by mistake and lose my weapon.

Keep it up! Can't wait to try it with quest 3 or pro in full color

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! There is a tool that can be used to define cover on the scene setup menu.  Automatic grip is a great idea and something I will consider putting in the game in the future.

reviews.review_description:
I’m having a blast, this game is SUPER fun! One of the best uses of mixed reality I’ve ever seen and It really showcases it’s potential. 

The setup and mapping is as easy as it can be even though the quest 2’s tracking and restrictions make things a little difficult. 

And the first time I played, I was immediately jump scared by the very realistic looking enemies spawning behind me, the immersion is real 😂

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! I am glad you enjoyed FPS Enhanced Reality!

reviews.review_description: for someone who loves trying new games this wasn’t it.it was very glitchy and bad. save you money

developer_response.body:
If you had purchased this game in the past for Quest 1.  Quest 1 is not a supported headset for this game, and will not be supported in the future.  Users have complained about the Quest 1 performance being unoptimized and buggy due to the limits of the device.

Meta has allowed developers a few months ago to remove the ability for Quest 1 support as a requirement.  If you have a Quest 1 you will not have access to purchase the game. Id recommend to upgrade to the Quest 2 or Pro and the game should transfer if you bought it before.

Quest 2 and Pro are being continually supported and improved with specific player feedback.

Quest 1 users had the opportunity to refund the game within a 2 week time window of their purchase if they were not happy about the games performance on the device.

reviews.review_description: best use for mixed reality. I wanna try it in a bigger space, my room's not so big but not too small:P eiether way i have so much fun with this game  :D

developer_response.body: I am glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description: This game is very good and gets your heart pumping a lil i would recommend it! Quite easy to set up and play okay baaii (very good game)

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! I really appreciate it!

reviews.review_description: Its a good game but I hate how everything shifts back before each game you play.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review,  I am working on a solution.  Spatial anchors are now available for developers.  I have to do some tests on these to see if they affect multifloor setup advanced settings and not interfere with the beyond room scale experience.  Since it is a manual process the walls may shift midgame if lighting is bad or the headset loses tracking.  Allowing the user to adjust the scene at the beginning of the game ensures that they can fix it at the start instead of going back to the scene setup, but I am working to make it easier in the coming updates.

reviews.review_description: It needs some patience to setup but afterwards it is really great mixed reality gaming experience.
The details in this game are unmatched. The dev team really went to another level with the amount of detail they put into this game, when you put on the headset, the guns look and feel real.
But when you buy this game I had a very hard time setting it up but I’m sped
This game is awesome sadly I have a quest 2 so I don’t get color but still amazing

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review!

reviews.review_description: ok so ur probably wondering why I gave this a one star review… the thing is I love this game but I have a very important question (also I will change my review to 5 stars I just want to make sure you see this). So I set up my whole house with 3 floors and everything seemed to be going great. But I start the game and all of the enemies spawned on top of my bed!!! Not a single one went off of it or downstairs! I also checked the basement and none were there either! So they were all spawning on top of my bed for no reason… I was wondering could you make a system where you can choose where the enemy’s spawn?? Like in the place where you build the walls you can add a place where it says enemy spawn point and choose where to put it so this bug doesn’t happen?!! I love this game but I can’t play it when all the enemy’s spawn in one place. Thanks for ur time and btw im on quest 3

developer_response.body: Hi There, thank you for pointing this out,  this is definitely something that I can try to fix through adjustments to the AI settings.  This occurs when the height of the wall covering the bed was too low so the enemy spawner thought that it was the floor. Enemies spawned on top of the bed trapping the enemies because they are not able to jump down.  I will reach out to your through the oculus app to try to help.  To prevent this from happening , currently the furniture must be above 1.1 meters tall until I have the chance to make the update.  I created a Discord server which can be found on the games website for help and feedback.  Cheers!

reviews.review_description: The game is funny but enemies are always behind wall and setup for a big house takes to long and guns are are cool wish their where more melee weapon like bats crowbars guitars also HAND TRACKING pls

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review!  To help with enemy behavior, I added a aggressive AI button where the enemies will be less likely to get stuck behind a wall.  Custom mode the enemies patrol a larger area. I plan on adding more weapons soon!

reviews.review_description:
I can't play the game, because of the unclear instructions. For starting the game, I have to turn on the gardian.  But when I want to setup the walls, the quest will stop the game as soon as I leave the guardian. The tutorial video is confusing, as it claims the guardian was turned off just for the video?!

Edit:  ok, one have to disable the gardian in the developer settings.

developer_response.body: Hi There, Please maximize your room scale guardian and draw the largest size possible in the center of your play area.  I will reach out to you to through the meta app to help you play the game.  You cannot go outside the meta guardian unless it you disable it.  I am working on auto setup so this should make it much easier.

reviews.review_title: AMAZING! Immersive AF Great for Training
reviews.review_description: This is easily the best use of VR AR and pass through opportunity, I can turn any location I desire into a full FPS training combat simulator, with a level of immersion instantaneously factors higher than any other games that I've played Even if you're using an IR pass through. With the quest 3 coming out this is going to be top of the list on that device bet, fax, no printer, period.
With more polishing, better gunplay and gore, youve got yourselves the first enhanced reality game i would actually play

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review and the feedback, much appreciated!

Thank you  https://discord.gg/S2JNj2weBJ

reviews.review_description: To truely play this I feel as though you need developer mode, as you need to turn off your boundary to move around with this game. Good concept, great excecution, but a mention of the boundary issue should be made clear.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review,  in-order for the game to be approved on Applab I can not mention to turn on dev mode within the app itself.

reviews.review_title: This is the baby of the games of the nea
reviews.review_description:
First off, I love the concept!  This is the baby of the games of the near future.  

Pros:  
1.  Passthru shooting is fun.
2. Easy setup of walls and furniture (cube/wall placement) very intuitive. (Mandatory)
3. Best for larger play areas (more fun, especially with obstacles to hide behind).
4. Bullet holes in walls.

Cons:  (hopefully in update!!!)
1. Unable to grip the foregrip of the rifles (2 handed usage) and supporting hand (pistols).
2. My giant controllers show up when i'm holding the gun (a little distracting).
3. Need masked "intruders" skins.
4. Walls skins (unless passthru comes in color)
5. Backpack and accessory loading needs help (see pavlov for setup).
6. Need doors and windows that open.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback!  Working on improving the ammo and backpack access in the next iterations.  I have been thinking of adding an ammo vest for quick access rather than the pop up menu.  Planning on making general fixes to the grips in the future.   Doors would be a cool feature!  I am looking forward to adding environmental scanning in the future as another option for the setup.

reviews.review_title: The app simply does not work on the MQ3
reviews.review_description:
Update: talked to the dev on "Discord" and he promptly helped me figure this out. Readily available to help.

Old post: I click "realistic mode" after selecting "quest 3 auto setup" and a button saying "generate mesh" appears. I press it and the game crashes.

Great game but could be better.
Grabbing ammo is pretty hard when you are in the middle of a fight.
The gun play doesnt feel really good.
Enemy spawns are okay not that bad not that good.
6/10

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review, I am currently experimenting with the ammo grab setup, next iteration will be to change the ammo from the pop up menu that includes all of the ammo types to an ammo vest were the ammo holder changes depending on which gun the player is holding.  For the enemy spawns make sure there are no gaps in the walls so the enemies don't spawn outside them.   Let me know how the weapons can improve and I will be happy to improve them.

reviews.review_description: FPS Enhanced Reality is an awesome game! It was easy to install and configure the play area. I personally have a Meta Quest 2, and the game looks great in black and white. I’d definitely recommend getting the quest pro if you can… color pass through really improves the experience! Regardless, there is something very unique about running through your own home fending off invaders. This game goes great with Hauntify!!!
